title: Evaluation of Electrocardiogram Biometric Verification Models Based on Short Enrollment Time on Medical and Wearable Recorders
venue: 2021 International Carnahan Conference on Security Technology (ICCST)

method.input_representation: 15 fiducial features (e.g., QS, meanRR, PQ) for ML; 150-point segments centered on R peak for DL
method.training_strategy: Varied training sizes (50, 150, 250, 500 seconds) to evaluate enrollment efficiency

evaluation.context: Achieved by Decision Tree (DT) classifier on WeSAD dataset

evaluation_protocol: Binary classification using genuine samples and randomly selected imposter samples from the dataset.

reported_limitations: Previous studies often use small subject pools leading to overfitting
High enrollment time required by existing systems

usability.usability_issues: Long enrollment time is a barrier to public acceptance

key_contribution: Demonstrated that ECG verification can be effective with extremely short enrollment times (5s for DL models)
Compared performance across medical-grade (Holter) and wearable (RespiBAN) ECG devices
main_strength: Focus on the practical constraint of enrollment time
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack testing
Evaluation performed on static datasets rather than live real-world deployment
insights.notes_for_review: The paper highlights a trade-off: while ML (NB, DT) can achieve lower EER with more data, the DL model is significantly more efficient regarding the required enrollment window.
